examples.en: I was about to call you.
I was about to leave when it started raining.
I was about to say the same thing.
examples.ko: 나도 막 같은 말 하려던 참이었어.

examples.en: She was about to cry.
We were about to order when they arrived.
I'd rather walk than wait for the bus.
I'd rather stay home than go to the party.
I'd rather tell the truth than lie.
I'd rather starve than eat that.
I'd rather die than give up.
It turns out that he already knew.
examples.ko: 알고 보니 그는 이미 알고 있었어.

examples.en: It turns out that the rumor was false.

examples.en: It turns out that she's my neighbor.
It turns out that I was wrong.
It turns out that they were dating.
I can't help but laugh.
I can't help but worry about you.
examples.ko: 네 걱정을 하지 않을 수가 없어.

examples.en: I can't help but wonder why.
examples.ko: 왜 그런지 궁금해하지 않을 수가 없어.

examples.en: I can't help but agree with her.
I can't help but smile when I see him.
examples.ko: 걔를 보면 미소 짓지 않을 수가 없어.

examples.en: There's no way he forgot my birthday.
examples.ko: 걔가 내 생일을 잊었을 리가 없어.

examples.en: There's no way she'd say that.
examples.ko: 그녀가 그런 말을 했을 리가 없어.

examples.en: There's no way this is real.

I might as well finish it now.
I might as well tell you the truth.

examples.en: Since we're here, we might as well eat.
examples.ko: 여기 온 김에, 그냥 먹는 게 낫겠어.

examples.en: I might as well give it a try.
We might as well leave now.
What if it rains tomorrow?
What if we get lost?
What if she says no?
What if we tried a different way?
What if I fail?
The thing is, I don't have time.
The thing is, I already made plans.

examples.en: The thing is, nobody told me.

examples.en: The thing is, it's more complicated than that.

examples.en: The thing is, I can't afford it.
examples.ko: 문제는 말이야, 나 그거 살 형편이 안 돼.

examples.en: I keep forgetting his name.
I keep making the same mistake.
I keep checking my phone.
I keep thinking about it.
My computer keeps crashing.
It's not that I'm angry, it's just I'm tired.
examples.ko: 화난 게 아니라, 그냥 피곤한 거야.

examples.en: It's not that I don't like it, it's just too expensive.
examples.ko: 싫은 게 아니라, 그냥 너무 비싼 거야.

examples.en: It's not that I forgot, it's just I was busy.
examples.ko: 잊은 게 아니라, 그냥 바빴던 거야.

examples.en: It's not that she's mean, it's just shy.
examples.ko: 그녀가 못된 게 아니라, 그냥 수줍은 거야.

examples.en: It's not that I don't care, it's just overwhelming.
examples.ko: 관심이 없어서가 아니라, 그냥 너무 벅찬 거야.

examples.en: I doubt he'll show up.
I doubt it will work.
I doubt she remembers me.
examples.ko: 그녀가 날 기억할 것 같지 않아.

examples.en: I doubt we'll finish on time.
examples.ko: 우리가 제시간에 끝낼 것 같지 않아.

examples.en: I doubt it's true.
Chances are she's already left.
Chances are it'll rain later.
Chances are he forgot.
Chances are they're stuck in traffic.
Chances are you're right.
I was wondering if you could help me.
examples.ko: 혹시 저 좀 도와주실 수 있나 해서요.

examples.en: I was wondering if you're free tonight.
examples.ko: 혹시 오늘 밤 시간 있으신가 해서요.

examples.en: I was wondering if I could ask a favor.
examples.ko: 혹시 부탁 하나 드려도 될까 해서요.

examples.en: I was wondering if it's still available.
I was wondering if we could reschedule.
examples.ko: 혹시 일정을 다시 잡을 수 있을까 해서요.

examples.en: As far as I know, the store is open.
examples.ko: 내가 아는 한, 그 가게는 열려 있어.

examples.en: As far as I know, she's still single.
examples.ko: 내가 아는 한, 그녀는 아직 싱글이야.

examples.en: As far as I know, nothing has changed.
examples.ko: 내가 아는 한, 바뀐 건 없어.

examples.en: As far as I know, he's on vacation.
examples.ko: 내가 아는 한, 걔는 휴가 중이야.

examples.en: As far as I know, the meeting is at 2.
It's about time we left.
It's about time you got a job.
examples.ko: 이제 슬슬 너도 취직할 때가 됐지.

examples.en: It's about time they fixed this.
It's about time we talked.
examples.ko: 우리 슬슬 얘기 좀 할 때가 됐어.

examples.en: It's about time you apologized.
I'm not really into horror movies.
I'm not really into sports.
I'm not really into cooking.
I'm not really into that kind of music.
I'm not really into parties.
You'd better hurry up.

You'd better not be late.
You'd better take an umbrella.
You'd better save your work.
I ended up staying home all day.
I ended up buying it anyway.
We ended up walking home.
I ended up missing the train.
We ended up talking for hours.
Let's just say it didn't go well.
Let's just say I learned my lesson.
Let's just say she wasn't happy.
Let's just say it's complicated.
Let's just say I've seen better.
examples.ko: 그냥 더 나은 것도 봤다고 해두자.

examples.en: I guess I could try it.
I guess I could help you out.
I guess I could stay a bit longer.
examples.ko: 뭐, 좀 더 있을 수도 있지.

examples.en: I guess I could give you a ride.
I guess I could ask him.
No matter what happens, I'll be there.
No matter what you say, I won't change my mind.
examples.ko: 네가 무슨 말을 해도, 내 마음은 안 바뀌어.

examples.en: No matter what, don't give up.
No matter what they think, be yourself.
No matter what it takes, we'll finish.
examples.ko: 무슨 수를 써서라도, 우린 끝낼 거야.

examples.en: I'm supposed to meet her at 3.

I'm supposed to call him back.
It doesn't matter if we're late.
It doesn't matter if you win or lose.
It doesn't matter if it's expensive.
It doesn't matter if he comes or not.
It doesn't matter if you don't understand yet.

pattern: I'll see what I can do

examples.en: I'll see what I can do about your request.
examples.ko: 네 요청에 대해 내가 할 수 있는지 알아볼게.

examples.en: No promises, but I'll see what I can do.
examples.ko: 장담은 못 하지만, 할 수 있는지 알아볼게.

examples.en: Leave it with me, I'll see what I can do.
I'll see what I can do to speed things up.
examples.ko: 좀 더 빨리 되게 할 수 있는지 알아볼게.

examples.en: Let me see what I can do for you.
examples.ko: 널 위해 뭘 할 수 있을지 알아볼게.

examples.en: That reminds me of my childhood.
examples.ko: 그거 보니까 내 어린 시절 생각나네.

examples.en: Your smile reminds me of your mom.

I'm kind of disappointed.
I'm kind of into her.

Feel free to look around.
Feel free to leave a comment.

I take it that you two have met.

examples.en: I take it that everything went well.
I take it that you don't agree.

This book is worth reading.
It's worth trying at least once.
examples.ko: 적어도 한 번쯤은 해볼 가치가 있어.

examples.en: The view is worth the hike.

That museum is worth visiting.

examples.en: I have a feeling that he's lying.

examples.en: I have a feeling that today's going to be a good day.
examples.ko: 왠지 오늘 좋은 날일 것 같아.

examples.en: I have a feeling that something's wrong.
I can't even boil water, let alone cook a meal.

I don't have time to sleep, let alone exercise.

I can't afford a car, let alone a house.

There's a chance that it'll snow tonight.

examples.en: I can't afford to miss this deadline.
I can't afford to lose my job.
I can't afford to take a day off.
I can't afford to make a mistake now.
I can't afford to waste time.
It just so happens that I know him.

I'd appreciate it if you could reply soon.

I'd appreciate it if you could help.
I'd appreciate it if you let me know.
I'd appreciate it if you were on time.
On second thought, let's not go there.

On second thought, I'd rather stay.
I'm used to working late.
I'm used to the noise now.
I'm used to living alone.
She's used to public speaking.

examples.en: I'm not used to waking up this early.

examples.en: It occurred to me that I forgot my keys.
examples.ko: 문득 열쇠를 놓고 왔다는 생각이 들었어.

examples.en: It occurred to me that she might be right.

examples.ko: 문득 너한테 고맙다는 말을 못 했다는 생각이 들었어.

examples.en: It never occurred to me that he'd say no.

examples.en: I might as well have stayed home.
I might as well have walked; the traffic was terrible.
examples.ko: 차가 너무 막혀서, 차라리 걸을걸 그랬어.

examples.en: Given that he's new, he did well.

examples.en: I wouldn't say it's the best, but it's good.

I wouldn't say it was easy.
I wouldn't say I hate it.

Right? That's what I'm saying.
Yes, that's exactly what I'm saying.
See? That's what I've been saying.

examples.en: Actually, I take that back.
I take that back, you were right.
examples.ko: 방금 한 말 취소, 네가 맞았어.

examples.en: Okay, I take it back. Sorry.
I take that back; it's actually pretty good.
examples.ko: 방금 말 취소, 사실 꽤 괜찮네.

examples.en: I shouldn't have said that. I take it back.

examples.en: For what it's worth, I think you did great.

examples.en: For what it's worth, he apologized to me too.

examples.en: For what it's worth, I'd choose you.

examples.en: I hate to say it, but you were right.
examples.ko: 이런 말 하기 싫지만, 네가 맞았어.

examples.en: I hate to say it, but we're out of time.
examples.ko: 이런 말 하기 싫지만, 시간이 다 됐어.

examples.en: I hate to say it, but it's not working.

examples.en: I hate to say it, but he has a point.
examples.ko: 인정하기 싫지만, 걔 말도 일리가 있어.

examples.en: I hate to say it, but we need to cancel.
I'm dying to see that movie.
I'm dying to try that new restaurant.
examples.ko: 그 새 식당 너무 가보고 싶어.

examples.en: I'm dying to know what happened.
I'm dying to go on vacation.
I'm dying for a cup of coffee.
examples.ko: 커피 한 잔 너무 마시고 싶어.

examples.en: It beats me why he did that.
It beats me why she's still upset.
examples.ko: 그녀가 왜 아직도 화나 있는지 모르겠어.

examples.en: It beats me why this isn't working.
examples.ko: 이게 왜 안 되는지 도무지 모르겠어.

examples.en: It beats me why they canceled.
It beats me how he passed.

pattern: I'll take your word for it

examples.en: Okay, I'll take your word for it.
I've never been there, but I'll take your word for it.
examples.ko: 나 거기 안 가봤지만, 네 말 믿을게.

examples.en: If you say so, I'll take your word for it.
I'll take your word for it this time.
I can't check now, so I'll take your word for it.
examples.ko: 지금 확인 못 하니까, 네 말 믿을게.

examples.en: Speaking of which, did you call her back?

examples.en: Speaking of which, where's my charger?

examples.en: Speaking of which, are you still coming tonight?
examples.ko: 말 나온 김에, 오늘 밤 아직 오는 거지?

examples.en: Speaking of which, I owe you money.
examples.ko: 말 나온 김에, 나 너한테 돈 갚아야 하지.

examples.en: I'm not one to complain, but this is too much.

examples.en: I'm not one to gossip.
examples.ko: 나 남 얘기 하는 사람 아니야.

examples.en: I'm not one to give up easily.
I'm not one to judge.
examples.ko: 나 남 함부로 판단하는 사람 아니야.

examples.en: I'm not one to brag, but I aced it.
examples.ko: 자랑하는 건 아닌데, 나 완벽하게 해냈어.

examples.en: It's not like we have a choice.
It's not like I did it on purpose.
It's not like she'll notice.
It's not like it's the end of the world.
It's not like you told me.
Thanks for the help, I owe you one.
You saved me. I owe you one.
I owe you one for covering my shift.
Seriously, I owe you big time.
Next round's on me. I owe you one.
examples.ko: 다음 한 잔은 내가 살게. 신세 졌어.

pattern: I'll get back to you on that

examples.en: Let me check and I'll get back to you on that.

I'm not sure yet, I'll get back to you on that.
examples.ko: 아직 잘 모르겠어, 그건 다시 알려줄게.

examples.en: Give me a day and I'll get back to you on that.
I'll get back to you on the price.
That being said, we should still try.
It's expensive. That being said, it's worth it.
examples.ko: 비싸긴 해. 그렇긴 해도, 그만한 값어치는 있어.

examples.en: That being said, I understand your point.

examples.ko: 걔 재능 있어. 그렇긴 해도, 연습은 필요해.

examples.en: That being said, let's give it a shot.
I couldn't agree more with your point.
Exactly! I couldn't agree more.
I couldn't agree more; it's about time.
Honestly, I couldn't agree more.
I couldn't agree more with what she said.

pattern: I'll take that as a yes

examples.en: No answer? I'll take that as a yes.
You're smiling, so I'll take that as a yes.
I'll take that as a yes, then.
Silence? I'll take that as a yes.
You nodded, so I'll take that as a yes.
Sorry, it completely slipped my mind.
I meant to call, but it slipped my mind.
Your birthday totally slipped my mind.
It slipped my mind that we had a meeting.
I'm sorry, the appointment slipped my mind.
I'm all for taking a break.
I'm all for it.
I'm all for trying something new.
examples.ko: 새로운 거 해보는 거 완전 찬성이야.

examples.en: I'm all for saving money.
I'm all for ordering pizza tonight.

examples.en: Let's face it, we're not ready yet.

examples.en: Let's face it, nobody's perfect.

pattern: I'll leave it up to you

examples.en: I don't mind either way, I'll leave it up to you.

examples.en: It's your call, I'll leave it up to you.

examples.en: I'll leave it up to you to decide.
